user_prompt: implement `void test_nvsloadsave()`, start the serial port, and load up BtHostList and WifiManager from NVS, read all the data items to Serial port, then write a the maximum amount of data (use the alphabet to fill up the strings) and then save them
I need to know if the NVS works or if we've exceeded some sort of size limit or need some compile time partitioning trickery
agent_reply: Implemented [test_nvsloadsave.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_nvsloadsave.cpp).
What it does:
- Starts Serial at `115200`.
- Prints NVS stats before load, after save, and after verify.
- Loads `WifiManager` and `BtHostList` from NVS.
- Dumps the full fixed-size tables to Serial, including passwords because this is explicitly a diagnostic test.
- Fills max-sized strings with repeated alphabet data.
- Writes max-size `network_cfg_t` and `bt_host_list_t` data to NVS.
- Reloads both managers and dumps the verified result.
- Spins forever at the end.

One note: `WifiManager` still applies current security-level policy during sanitize, so counts like AP/cloud allowed entries may clamp, but the fixed arrays are filled before the full struct blob is saved.

Verification: I ran `pio run -e thefly_sec1`. The new test file compiled; the build failed later at link with the existing `.iram0.text` overflow by `2104 bytes`, not from this code. The build also regenerated [web_assets.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h) because the package script ran.

agent_reply: Fixed the bootloop cause in [test_nvsloadsave.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_nvsloadsave.cpp:229).

The crash looked like it happened in `Serial.printf`, but the real issue was stack pressure: `test_nvsloadsave()` had multiple large `WifiManager` / `network_cfg_t` / `BtHostList` objects as locals, so the setup task stack was already getting smashed before the first diagnostic print finished.
I changed it so:
- large report/test buffers are static storage
- `WifiManager` / `BtHostList` objects are allocated with `new`
- the first huge capacity print is split into smaller prints
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`: build succeeded. The build regenerated [web_assets.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h) again as part of the existing web asset packaging step.

user_prompt: edit the code so that the huge chunk of data is always using a global instance that's allocated on... I think BSS? make it guaranteed not to crash no matter how we instantiate
agent_reply: Done. I moved all the big diagnostic state in [test_nvsloadsave.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_nvsloadsave.cpp) to file-scope globals:
- `network_cfg_t` report buffer
- max-size `network_cfg_t`
- `bt_host_list_t` report buffer
- both `WifiManager` instances
- both `BtHostList` instances
So the large backing storage is zero-initialized static storage, effectively `.bss`, instead of setup-task stack or heap. I also removed the `new` path entirely.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec0`: build succeeded. The web asset packaging step regenerated `inc/web_assets.h` again during the build.
